# Let me parse the map more carefully to understand my position relative to the stairs
lines = obs.split('\n')
# Find map section
start = lines.index('--- MAP ---') + 1
map_lines = lines[start:start+25]
# The @ position at (10,7) - we need to find which line that appears on
for i, ln in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '@' in ln:
        # Find the column of @
        at_col = ln.index('@')
        print(f"Line {i}: '@ at column {at_col}")
        print(f"Content: {ln}")
        print(f"Lines below:")
        for j in range(i+1, min(i+5, len(map_lines))):
            print(f"Line {j}: {map_lines[j]}")
        print(f"Lines above:")
        for j in range(max(0, i-3), i):
            print(f"Line {j}: {map_lines[j]}")
        break

# Check what's directly south - look at character below @ on next line
for i, ln in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '@' in ln:
        at_col = ln.index('@')
        next_ln = map_lines[i+1]
        print(f"\nLine below @: {next_ln}")
        print(f"Character at col {at_col}: '{next_ln[at_col] if at_col < len(next_ln) else '?'}'")
        # Check a few positions around for >
        for j in range(max(0, at_col-5), min(len(next_ln), at_col+6)):
            if next_ln[j] == '>':
                print(f"Found > at line {i+1}, col {j}")

    # Calculate which direction to move
    if abs(dx) > abs(dy):
        # Move horizontally

            direction = 'E'
        else:

    else:

            direction = 'S'
        else:

    print(f"Initial direction to move: {direction}")
